title: Implement 5G NR LDPC code construction from 3GPP TS 38.212
description:
Replace the stub in `ldpc/nr_5g.rs` with a full 5G NR LDPC code construction from 3GPP TS 38.212.

## What to Implement

1. **Base matrices** — BG1 (46×68) and BG2 (42×52) from 3GPP TS 38.212 Tables 5.3.2-2 and 5.3.2-3. These are large tables of shift values defining the quasi-cyclic structure.
2. **Lifting size table** — `i_LS` to Z mapping from Table 5.3.2-1. Valid lifting sizes: Z ∈ {2,3,…,16} × {1,2,4,8,16,32,64,128,192,256,384} (with constraints).
3. **Factory method** — `QuasiCyclicLdpc::nr_5g(base_graph, lifting_factor)` that constructs the parity-check matrix.
4. **Rate matching** — systematic bit selection for a target (n,k) from the mother code, including shortening and puncturing.
5. **Filler bit handling** — when K doesn't fill an integer number of lifting groups.

## Target Codes

Needed by downstream simulations:

| (n, k) | Rate | BG |
|---|---|---|
| (256, 121) | 0.473 | BG2 |
| (256, 49) | 0.191 | BG2 |
| (625, 225) | 0.360 | BG2 |
| (1024, 441) | 0.431 | BG2 |
| (1024, 640) | 0.625 | BG1 or BG2 |
| (4096, 3249) | 0.793 | BG1 |

## Acceptance Criteria

- `nr_5g(1, z)` and `nr_5g(2, z)` produce valid LDPC codes for all standard Z values.
- Rate matching produces codes matching the target (n,k) dimensions above.
- BP decoding on constructed codes achieves expected BER/BLER performance.
- All existing LDPC tests continue to pass.
- The paper's exact construction parameters (Z, shortening/puncturing) are documented per target code, noting any ambiguities.

## Location

`crates/gf2-coding/src/ldpc/nr_5g.rs` (replace existing stub).
